message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: TTL feature for cache layers touches only cache.py and __init__.py exports. Please propose your files so we can confirm disjoint split.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implementing opt-in disk-layer compression (gzip/zlib) with magic-header format + adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) in configure_cache(). Need to own both cache.py and clients/__init__.py since compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params flow through configure_cache() into Cache constructor. If your feature also touches these files, let's split by function/section or I can apply your changes on top of mine.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Both features genuinely need to touch both files (Cache.__init__ signature + configure_cache signature), so file-level disjoint split isn't possible. Proposing sequencing instead: I'll go first since I've already scoped my diff (adding compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params + magic-header serialize/deserialize helpers, purely additive to Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures/docstrings). I will NOT touch memory_cache/LRUCache logic. I'll send DONE with a summary of exact signature/line changes when finished (should be quick). Please hold off editing until you see my DONE, then re-read the files fresh and layer your ttl/TTLCache changes on top - your changes to memory_cache logic won't conflict with mine (disk-only). If you'd rather go first, reply and I'll wait instead. Reply ACCEPT to confirm this ordering.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: We have genuine overlap: both features add opt-in kwargs to Cache.__init__ and configure_cache(), so a disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposal: we both own these 2 files, but keep edits additive/non-overlapping at the line level - I'll only touch: Cache.__init__ signature (add ttl param at the end, after your params if present), memory-cache construction (TTLCache vs LRUCache branch), Cache.get/put (ttl-based expire() call), configure_cache() signature (add ttl=None at the end), a new set_ttl() helper function, DSPY_CACHE instantiation, and __all__ (append 'set_ttl'). I will not touch compression/cull_limit/eviction_policy code. Before I edit, I'll read current file state and append my changes around whatever you've already added, rather than overwriting. Please do the same for my additions. Sequencing: I'll implement first since I proposed first - ping me when you start so we don't clash mid-edit, or go ahead in parallel and we'll each re-read-before-write. Accept?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Accepted. You go first (ttl param at end of Cache.__init__/configure_cache, memory-cache TTLCache branch, get/put expire() call, set_ttl(), __all__ append). I will not touch memory_cache/TTLCache/ttl code. I'll wait for your DONE, then re-read both files fresh and append my compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params + magic-header disk serialize/deserialize logic without disturbing your additions. Go ahead.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Accepted: you go first (compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy, disk-only). I'll wait for your DONE, re-read files fresh, then layer ttl/TTLCache/set_ttl on top. Will avoid touching your compression/serialize helpers and cull_limit/eviction_policy code.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Confirmed - I'll go first now (ttl/TTLCache/set_ttl additions). Will send DONE when finished so you can layer your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy changes on top.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Finished TTL feature. Summary of exact changes so you can layer on top: (1) cache.py: import TTLCache from cachetools; Cache.__init__ signature gained 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' as the LAST positional param (after memory_max_entries); stores self.ttl and self.memory_max_entries; memory_cache branch: TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl) if ttl is not None else LRUCache(...); added new method Cache.set_ttl(self, ttl) right before reset_memory_cache(); get() miss branch ('else: return None') now calls self.disk_cache.expire() first when enable_disk_cache and self.ttl is not None; put()'s disk-cache write branch now does 'if self.ttl is not None: self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=self.ttl) else: self.disk_cache[key] = value' (previously just self.disk_cache[key] = value - you'll want to build your compression logic around this new if/else, not replace it). (2) __init__.py: configure_cache() gained 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' as new LAST kwarg, forwarded positionally as 6th arg to Cache(...); DSPY_CACHE construction now passes ttl=None explicitly; added top-level set_ttl(seconds) function (calls dspy.cache.set_ttl(seconds)) placed right before enable_litellm_logging(); __all__ gained 'set_ttl' appended after 'configure_cache'. All tests pass (tests/clients/test_cache.py, tests/caching/test_litellm_cache.py) and ruff is clean. Go ahead with your changes - re-read both files fresh first.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Finished my changes, tests pass (existing tests/clients/test_cache.py all green + manual roundtrip/backward-compat/size checks). Summary of what I touched so you can layer on top safely: cache.py - added module-level constants/helpers (_DISK_BLOB_MAGIC, _CODEC_*, _validate_compress, _encode_disk_value, _decode_disk_value) right after the logger; Cache.__init__ signature gained compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy as the LAST 3 params (add your ttl param after these); added self.compress assignment and disk_cache_kwargs dict construction (conditionally adds cull_limit/eviction_policy) replacing the old inline FanoutCache(...) call; added new Cache.set_compression() method right after __init__; get() now calls _decode_disk_value(self.disk_cache[key]) instead of raw self.disk_cache[key]; put() now calls _encode_disk_value(value, self.compress) instead of raw value. I did NOT touch memory_cache/LRUCache construction or get/put's memory-cache branches - all yours. __init__.py - configure_cache() signature gained compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy as the last 3 params before the closing paren (add ttl after mine); docstring extended; Cache(...) call in configure_cache() now passes compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy as 3 new positional args at the end (add ttl after them, and update call + DSPY_CACHE instantiation together). Go ahead and re-read both files now.
